name: Shrimp Santorini

ingredients.item: shrimp, peeled and deveined
extra-virgin olive oil, divided
kosher salt, plus more for seasoning the onion
freshly ground black pepper, plus more for seasoning the shrimp
onion, chopped
garlic cloves, minced
tomatoes, chopped or grated
red pepper flakes
dried Greek oregano
Handful Kalamata olives, pitted (optional)
feta cheese
chopped fresh parsley
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a large bowl, toss the shrimp with 1 tablespoon of olive oil and the salt, and season with black pepper.
In a medium oven-safe skillet over medium heat, heat the remaining 4 tablespoons of olive oil.
Add the onion and season with salt. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes, until translucent.
Add the garlic and black pepper. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes, stirring, until soft.
Add the tomatoes, red pepper flakes, and oregano. Sauté for 10 minutes. (If your skillet isn’t oven-safe, you can turn the tomato mixture into a 9-by-13-inch baking dish.)
Arrange the shrimp and olives (if using) over the tomato mixture in one layer.
Crumble the feta over the surface.
Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, until the shrimp are a medium-pinkish color and the cheese is a bit browned. Don’t overcook the shrimp.
Remove from the oven and garnish with parsley.
